title: Disaster Related Volunteer Preparedness/Mobilization Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Volunteering & Donating > Disaster Related Volunteer Preparedness/Mobilization Programs 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIONAL VOLUNTARY ORGANIZATIONS ACTIVE IN DISASTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides disaster relief coordination between a diverse group of organizations serving communities throughout the disaster cycle. Volunteer opportunities are available. Does not provide direct assistance; households seeking assistance must contact local organizations. Website Alexandria, VA 22313 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (703) 778-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL VOLUNTARY ORGANIZATIONS ACTIVE IN DISASTER OREGON VOLUNTARY ORGANIZATIONS ACTIVE IN DISASTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides disaster relief coordination between a diverse group of organizations serving communities throughout the disaster cycle. Volunteer opportunities are available. Does not provide direct assistance; households seeking assistance must contact local organizations. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the State of Oregon roster of licensed medical professionals who have registered to volunteer in response to local, state, or federal emergencies. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: -Licensed physicians, nurses, pharmacists, Emergency Medical Technicians (EMTs), behavioral health providers, respiratory therapists, and other medical care providers. -Previously licensed medical professionals may be required to attend additional training, and a background check is required prior to activation. - Medical professionals licensed in another state; see website for volunteer requirements. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (877) 343-5767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGONSERVES OREGON VOLUNTEERS IN DISASTER (ORVID) Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online statewide disaster volunteer management and recruitment system. Volunteers can connect to opportunities to help in their communities. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON OREGONSERVES OREGON VOLUNTEERS IN DISASTER (ORVID)
